Since the lake currently has the maximum fish population of 100 tons, I want to catch a reasonable amount to maximize my income while ensuring the fish population can replenish for future months. If I catch too much, the fish population will decrease significantly, reducing future income for everyone. If I catch too little, I miss out on potential earnings this month.

If all five fishermen catch 20 tons each (total 100 tons), the lake will be depleted to 0 tons before reproduction, and no fish will reproduce, leading to no fish next month. This is not sustainable.

If each catches 10 tons (total 50 tons), 50 tons remain, which doubles to 100 tons after reproduction, maintaining the population.

To balance income and sustainability, I will choose to catch 10 tons this month.

Answer: 10
